events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Ringo Starr, drummer for The Beatles, was born Richard Starkey on July 7, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: Ringo's Big Day
angles.copy: He brought the beat to the biggest band in the world. Today, we're celebrating Ringo Starr turning 86! What's your favourite Beatles track featuring Ringo on drums or vocals?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Sir Brian May was born on July 19, 1947, in Hampton Hill, Middlesex.

angles.name: Rock God Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! From stadium-rocking anthems to epic guitar solos, what's your top Queen moment featuring the legendary guitarist?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Sir Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent.

angles.name: Jagger's Milestone
angles.copy: Still got the moves like Jagger at 83! Wishing a very happy birthday to the inimitable Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Rolling Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first music video.

angles.name: MTV's Legacy
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV changed music forever with its launch! Remember the first video ever played? 'Video Killed The Radio Star' by The Buggles. What was the first music video that truly blew your mind?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' released (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring classics like 'Live Forever' and 'Supersonic'.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' – an album that defined a generation. Which track from their iconic debut still gets you singing along the loudest?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a defining moment of 1960s counterculture, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Remembering Woodstock
angles.copy: 57 years ago, half a million people gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. What's the one performance you wish you could have seen live at that iconic festival?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death (1977)
events.blurb: The King of Rock & Roll, Elvis Presley, passed away on August 16, 1977, leaving an indelible mark on music history.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day 49 years ago, we lost a true legend. Remembering Elvis Presley, whose music continues to captivate generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Tupac Shakur shot in Las Vegas (1996)
events.blurb: On September 7, 1996, influential rapper Tupac Shakur was shot in Las Vegas, tragically passing away six days later.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years ago this week, the music world was rocked by the shooting of Tupac Shakur. His impact on hip-hop and culture remains immense. What's the Tupac track that resonates most with you?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death (1970)
events.blurb: Guitar icon Jimi Hendrix tragically passed away on September 18, 1970, at the age of 27.

angles.name: Hendrix's Enduring Sound
angles.copy: On this day 56 years ago, we lost a guitar pioneer. Remembering the unparalleled talent of Jimi Hendrix. What's your favourite track from the legend?

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' released (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', in the UK on September 26, 1969.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That iconic zebra crossing shot, those timeless tracks. Which side of the album is your favourite?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' formidable frontwoman, Chrissie Hynde, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Chrissie Hynde's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon, still delivering powerful vocals and attitude. What's your top Pretenders track?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis frontman and solo artist Liam Gallagher was born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester.

angles.name: Liam's Big Day
angles.copy: He's still got it! Wishing a very happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher. What's your favourite track from the Manchester icon?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic peace anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971.

angles.name: Imagine's Message
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon released 'Imagine'. A song that continues to inspire and challenge us to envision a better world. What does 'Imagine' mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on October 23, 2001, promising '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

angles.name: iPod Revolution
angles.copy: Remember when you could fit 1,000 songs in your pocket? 25 years ago, Apple introduced the first iPod and changed how we listen to music forever. What was your first iPod model?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From iconic gadgets to unforgettable theme songs, what's your favourite thing about the world of 007?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary co-founder of The Beatles, John Lennon, was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His music and message continue to inspire generations. What's your favourite Lennon song, with or without The Beatles?
